DISCOVERY 302, NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MICHAEL BERGMAN'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT SETS A HEARING FOR DECEMBER 11, 2012 AT 9:00 FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY TERMINATING SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE GRANTED IN THE EVENT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT FULLY COMPLIED WITH THE COURT'S ORDER ENTERED AUG 29, 2012 BEFORE THE HEARING DATE. ANY BRIEF OR RESPONSE IS LIMITED TO 5 PAGES, PLUS DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE, AND SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 1005(B). COURTESY COPIES ARE TO BE PROVIDED TO DEPARTMENT 302 AT THE TIME OF FILING. COURT TO PREPARE THE ORDER. ERNST HALPERIN, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

DISCOVERY 302, NO APPEARANCES AS TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY TERMINATING SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE GRANTED. RULING: THERE BEING NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: NO RESPONSE TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HAVING BEEN SUBMITTED, THE TERMINATING SANCTIONS REQUESTED BY DEFENDANT ARE GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 2023.030(D)(3). COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. THE ORDER SHALL DESCRIBE IN A SUMMARY FASHION THE DISCOVERY PROPOUNDED, THE MOTION TO COMPEL, PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO OPPOSE THE MOTION TO COMPEL, THE COURT'S ORDER DATED AUGUST 29, 2012, DEFENDANT'S FURTHER MOTION TO COMPEL AND REQUEST FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE MOTION, THE COURT'S NOVEMBER 9TH ORDER SETTING AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR DECEMBER 11TH AND PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. ERNST HALPERIN, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-15-2013 10:30 AM FOR DEPT 610

calendar.matters:
ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-17-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Jesse Kershner, Esq. for Defendant. No appearance by Plaintiff. Court ordered case dismissed without prejudice. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Order To Show Cause Why Terminating Sanctions Should Not Be Granted
Off Calendar Dec-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

Order To Show Cause Why Terminating Sanctions Should Not Be Granted

Ruling: There being no appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: No response to the Order to Show Cause having been submitted, the terminating sanctions requested by Defendant are GRANTED pursuant to CCP Section 2023.030(d)(3). Counsel for Defendant is to prepare an order. The order shall describe in a summary fashion the discovery propounded, the motion to compel, plaintiff's failure to oppose the motion to compel, the court's order dated August 29, 2012, Defendant's further motion to compel and request for terminating sanctions, plaintiff's failure to respond to the motion, the Court's November 9th order setting an order to show cause for December 11th and plaintiff's failure to respond to the order to show cause. Ernst Halperin, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2012 Continued To Jan-15-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL BERGMAN'S Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions is Denied without prejudice. The Court sets a hearing for December 11, 2012 at 9:00 for an order to show cause why terminating sanctions should not be granted in the event plaintiff has not fully complied with the court's order entered Aug 29, 2012 before the hearing date. Any brief or response is limited to 5 pages, plus documentary evidence, and shall be filed and served in accordance with CCP 1005(b). Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 at the time of filing. Court to prepare the order. Ernst Halperin, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Response And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT BERGMAN'S Motion To Compel Discovery Response And Request For Sanctions is Granted. No opposition filed. The court signed the proposed order provided with the courtesy copies as modified. Judge Pro Tem: Brad Kaplan; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
